Makes the settings page (and the Docker bubble) actually appear. This is the real fix behind the missing Settings entry; the v1.0.10 page rename did not address it.

## Fixed
- The package shipped its `.page` files with **CRLF** line endings (a Windows
  build artefact). Unraid's `PageBuilder` splits a page's header from its body on
  a pure-LF `---` separator, so a CRLF `.page` fails `parse_ini_string` and is
  silently dropped — no Settings entry, and no Docker-tab bubble. A CRLF shebang
  also breaks the engine's `rc.shiplog`.
- All plugin files now ship with **LF**, enforced two ways so a Windows checkout
  can't regress it: a `.gitattributes` (`eol=lf`) and an LF-normalise step in
  `pkg_build.sh`.

The display name stays **ShipLog**; the page is `shiplog.page` with
`launch="Settings/shiplog"` (slug-consistent, the FanCtrlPlus layout).
